courses.name: Language Matters: Introduction to ALDS
courses.description: Core topics in applied linguistics and discourse studies. First and second language acquisition; sign language; language teaching and assessment; language in society; language, identity and power; discourse analysis; written language and literacy.

courses.name: Analysis of Oral Language Use
courses.description: Introduction to the analysis of oral language in use; distinctions between spoken and written language; theoretical and methodological approaches such as speech act theory, ethnography of communication, conversation analysis, and discourse analysis; classroom interaction; interaction in first- and second-language acquisition; analysis of spoken language corpora.
courses.prerequisites: ALDS 1001 or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Analysis of Written Language Use
courses.description: Introduction to the analysis of written language in use, including theoretical and methodological approaches such as rhetorical genre studies (including academic and workplace writing); adult literacy studies; text-structure analysis; discourse analysis (including critical discourse analysis); analysis of textual corpora.
courses.prerequisites: ALDS 1001 or FYSM 1004 or COMS 1001 or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Linguistic Theory and Second-Language Learning
courses.description: Critical study of linguistic theory and description applied to second-language learning; a brief consideration of similarities and differences in first- and second-language development, bilingualism and types of linguistic error and their significance.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing.

courses.name: Strategies for Successful Writers
courses.description: Strategies for successful academic and professional writing with an emphasis on audience awareness, purpose, and context of writing. Approaches to peer review and time management for enhanced writing productivity. Practice with tools for the development of academic and professional text types.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Communication Differences and Disabilities I
courses.description: A survey course highlighting a variety of communication differences and disabilities. Specific topics vary from year to year but typically will include speech, language, fluency and hearing differences and disabilities.
courses.prerequisites: LING 1001 and second-year standing, or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Bilingualism
courses.description: The linguistic nature of bilingualism. The structure of bilingual societies and the relation between societal and individual bilingualism. The role of bilingualism in language education.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing.

courses.name: Language and Power
courses.description: How social conditions engender different linguistic choices. Attention to linguistic resources for expressing ideological beliefs and for maintaining and reinforcing power structures in institutional and social sites.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing.

courses.name: Intercultural Communication
courses.description: Introduction to intercultural communication with an emphasis on social interaction, multimodality, and identity construction. Application of theoretical perspectives to case studies through empirical inquiry and storytelling. Specific topics include cultural identity and food, gesture and nonverbal communication, and the structure of rhetoric.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing, and one of LING 1001 or ALDS 1001 , or permission of the School.

courses.name: Sociolinguistics
courses.description: The place of language within society; bilingual and multilingual communities; language, social mobility and social stratification; sociolinguistic factors in language change.
courses.prerequisites: ALDS 1001 and third-year standing.

courses.name: English as a Global Language
courses.description: The origins, development and globalization of the English language. Establishment of Standard English; spread of English in the Inner circle and in expanding circles; world Englishes; linguistic features of English varieties. English as a global language; learning and teaching English as an international language.
courses.prerequisites: ALDS 1001 and LING 1001 .

courses.name: Introduction to Deaf Studies
courses.description: A critical introduction to Deaf community and culture as they relate to a social model of disability, to ethnicity, and to issues of diversity and inclusion. Discourse analysis of research and policy in education for Deaf students from early childhood and beyond.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing in Linguistics or Applied Linguistics and Discourse Studies or enrolment in the Minor in Disability Studies.

courses.name: Research and Theory in Academic Writing
courses.description: Study of contemporary research and theory (1970s to present) on academic writing in elementary, secondary and post-secondary school, with emphasis on writing in university. Consideration of what academic writing entails, how writing fosters learning, and how instruction can help students develop their writing abilities.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Research and Theory in Workplace Writing
courses.description: Study of contemporary research and theory (1980s to present) in writing in workplace settings. Consideration of how writing is used in accomplishing work, how novices learn to write effectively, and what the implications are for pedagogy.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Second Language Writing
courses.description: Theory and practice of second language (L2) writing: how people learn to write in a second language, and how L2 writing courses for specific groups of learners can be designed.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing in Applied Linguistics and Discourse Studies, or in Linguistics, or enrolment in the CTESL program, or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Introduction to Professional Writing and Editing
courses.description: The fundamental skills of professional writing and editing, including writing for specific audiences, document design, revision strategies, copyediting.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Communication Differences and Disabilities II
courses.description: An in-depth examination of select topics in the field of communication differences and disabilities. An emphasis is placed on theoretical accounts of specific differences and disabilities and the cross-linguistic evidence for these accounts. Specific topics may vary from year to year.
courses.prerequisites: LING 1001 and one of ALDS 2604 or LING 2604 .

courses.name: Corpus Linguistics
courses.description: Computer-assisted analysis of electronic collections of naturally occurring language.  Applications in such areas as language variation, grammar, lexicology, phraseology, translation, and learner language.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing in Applied Linguistics and Discourse Studies, or in Linguistics, or enrolment in the CTESL program, or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Adult Literacy
courses.description: The extent and social contexts of restricted literacy in Canadian society; approaches to and debates surrounding the teaching and learning of adult literacy.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Discourse Analysis
courses.description: Principles of and studies in discourse analysis, including both conversational and textual/documentary analysis. The major focus is on language use in structuring social relationships.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing in Applied Linguistics and Discourse Studies, or in Linguistics, or enrolment in the CTESL program, or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Beyond the BA
courses.description: Students explore personal and professional transitions from undergraduate to entering the workforce or graduate school. Topics may include self-assessments, career management skills, and networking. Both academic and practical work, featuring interaction from career specialists, graduate schools, professionals, and employed ALDS graduates.
courses.prerequisites: Third-year standing in ALDS or LING or permission of the School.

courses.name: Introduction to Forensic Linguistics
courses.description: Study of language use as legal evidence and in court proceedings. Consideration of oral, written, and multimodal linguistic evidence in a variety of forensic contexts including authorship profiling, asylum seeking, plagiarism, police interviews, etc. Application of selected data analysis methods to real-world forensic linguistic data.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing or permission of instructor.

courses.name: Independent Study
courses.description: Research under the supervision of a member of the School. Normally available only to third- and fourth-year students in Applied Linguistics and Discourse Studies.
courses.prerequisites: permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Independent Study
courses.description: Research under the supervision of a member of the School. Normally available only to third- and fourth-year students in Applied Linguistics and Discourse Studies.
courses.prerequisites: permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Special Topic in Applied Linguistics and Discourse Studies
courses.description: Selected topics in Applied Linguistics and Discourse Studies not ordinarily treated in the regular course program.

courses.name: Language Assessment and Testing
courses.description: The principles of test construction as applied to testing language proficiency, achievement and aptitude. Structural, notional, discrete point and integrative tests. Diagnostic assessment of language development, language disorders, and literacy. Students are expected to create, analyze and evaluate language tests.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing in Applied Linguistics and Discourse Studies, or in Linguistics, or enrolment in the CTESL program, or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Methods and Practice in Language Pedagogy
courses.description: Integrates theory and description of language learning and teaching with practical work in one of the languages offered by the School. Requires observation in a language classroom, along with practical work facilitating in-class or language lab activities, or developing teaching materials.
courses.prerequisites: permission of the language instructor for the language class in which practical work will be conducted; proficiency in the language in question, as determined by either completion of the prerequisites for 4010 of that language, or assessment by the language instructor; or permission of the School.

courses.name: Practicum in Teaching ESL
courses.description: Investigates the processes of classroom learning with observation and some teaching experience in ESL classes. Normally taken concurrently with and .
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in the concurrent CTESL program, or enrolment in the post-graduate CTESL program.

courses.name: ESL Literacy
courses.description: The nature of everyday literacy and literacy skills. Analyzing the structure of everyday literacy texts and demands. Issues in literacy for second-language learners.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing in Applied Linguistics and Discourse Studies, or in Linguistics, or enrolment in the CTESL program, or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Languages for Specific Purposes
courses.description: An introduction to Languages for Specific Purposes  - language instruction tailored to specific groups of learners, e.g. English for Science, for Business, for the Workplace, for Academic Purposes. Research and teaching methodology. Emphasis on EAP/ESP research and instruction at Carleton.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing in Applied Linguistics and Discourse Studies, or in Linguistics, or enrolment in the CTESL program, or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Teaching English as a Foreign Language: Methodology for Global Contexts
courses.description: An introduction to the principles of teaching language in a foreign-language context; review of teaching approaches; practical examination, development and evaluation of instructional materials.
courses.prerequisites: ALDS 4305 and fourth-year standing in the concurrent CTESL program, enrolment in the post-graduate CTESL program, the BGInS Specialization in Teaching English in Global Contexts, or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Teaching English Language: Methodology I
courses.description: Classification of classroom teaching methods and materials; adaptation of teaching materials for particular situations; creation of teaching materials; teaching techniques and strategies.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in the concurrent CTESL program, enrolment in the post-graduate CTESL program, or the BGInS Specialization in Teaching English in Global Contexts, or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Teaching English as a Second Language: Methodology II
courses.description: Classification of classroom teaching methods and materials used in an international context; adaptation of teaching materials for particular situations; creation of teaching materials for global English language education; teaching techniques and strategies.
courses.prerequisites: ALDS 4305 and fourth-year standing in the concurrent CTESL program, enrolment in the post-graduate CTESL program, or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: English for Specific Purposes
courses.description: An introduction to English for Specific Purposes – English language instruction tailored to specific groups of learners (e.g., English for Academic Purposes, and English for a range of specific occupational and professional purposes). This course explores effective practices in course and materials design.
courses.prerequisites: ALDS 2203 or ALDS 4602 and third-year standing in the BGInS Honours Specialization in Teaching English in Global Contexts, or enrolment in the CTESL program, or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Writing and Knowledge-Making in the Disciplines
courses.description: The role of writing in constructing knowledge in academic disciplines, as viewed from contemporary socio-cultural perspectives. Consideration of how the goals, values, and assumptions of different disciplines shape their writing in distinctive ways and what implications this holds for pedagogy.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Writing and Knowledge-Making in the Professions
courses.description: The role of writing in constructing knowledge in the professions, as viewed from contemporary socio-cultural perspectives. How the goals, values, and assumptions of different professions shape their writing in distinctive ways and the implications for theory, research, and practice.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Teaching Writing in School and the Workplace
courses.description: Introduction to approaches for teaching writing in elementary and secondary school, in university, and in the workplace, with a focus on socio-cultural theories of language and learning. Discussion of applications of these approaches to classroom and workplace teaching.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing, or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Second Language Acquisition
courses.description: Current issues in second language acquisition; factors influencing success in acquiring a second or additional language, discourse and culture. Emphasis on theoretical concepts, empirical research, and practical implications for language teaching.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing in Applied Linguistics and Discourse Studies, or in Linguistics, or enrolment in the CTESL program, or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Statistics for Language Research
courses.description: Application of statistical procedures to analysis of language data and to problems of measurement in experimental linguistics, applied linguistics, psycholinguistics, and related fields.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing in Linguistics or Applied Linguistics and Discourse Studies or Cognitive Science, or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Systemic-Functional Linguistics
courses.description: Functions of language in the exchange of meanings between people in a wide variety of communicative situations. Semantic and syntactic resources at risk in these different contexts. Interactions between language and the social context.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing in Applied Linguistics and Discourse Studies, or Linguistics, or Journalism, or Communication Studies, or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Major Structures of English
courses.description: This course is intended to familiarize students with the structure of the English language, highlighting important contrasts between English and other languages as well as grammatical difficulties for ESL learners.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing in Applied Linguistics and Discourse Studies, or in Linguistics, or enrolment in the CTESL program, or permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Independent Study
courses.description: Permits fourth-year Honours students to pursue their interests in a selected area of applied linguistics and discourse studies.
courses.prerequisites: permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Independent Study
courses.description: Permits fourth-year Honours students to pursue their interests in a selected area of applied linguistics and discourse studies.
courses.prerequisites: permission of the instructor.

courses.name: Special Topic in Applied Linguistics and Discourse Studies
courses.description: Selected topics in applied linguistics and discourse studies. Contents of this course vary from year to year.

courses.name: Honours Project in Applied Linguistics and Discourse Studies
courses.description: Individually designed intensive practicum or research experience. May involve (a) practicum or work study placement in writing or literacy studies, language syllabus design or test development; (b) intensive research activity in an area of Applied Linguistics and Discourse Studies. All projects include substantial written work.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in Applied Linguistics and Discourse Studies, a CGPA of 9.00 or better, or permission of the School.
